incident.summary: A 25-year-old contracted worker, Jose Luis Bautista of Donna, Texas, died on 15 May 2026 after falling approximately eight feet from scaffolding at SpaceX's Starbase construction facility near Brownsville, Texas; he was pronounced dead at a hospital at 5:15 a.m. OSHA confirmed its investigation on 18 May 2026, and multiple media outlets reported the story through the window period of 19-22 May 2026, as SpaceX declined to comment and Starbase city officials deferred all queries to the company. SpaceX has a documented history of elevated injury rates at Starbase — nearly six times the industry average for comparable facilities per 2024 OSHA data — and has accumulated 17 OSHA citations since 2021.
incident.severity_rationale: A worker fatality with confirmed OSHA investigation at a high-profile facility already subject to 17 prior citations; the pattern of elevated injury rates and the company's non-cooperation with media elevates both regulatory and reputational severity.

secondary_mappings.why: OSHA has formally opened a fatality investigation under the OSH Act; SpaceX faces potential citations and penalties on a site with an existing enforcement history.
The deceased was a contracted worker; the incident exposes SpaceX's contractor oversight and site safety management for contracted labour.
SpaceX's refusal to comment and its pattern of prior safety violations amplified adverse media coverage across national and regional outlets during the window.
Fatal fall from scaffolding constitutes a physical workplace safety incident causing death on company premises.

contextual_vendors.name: Intelex Technologies (part of ETQ)
contextual_vendors.product: EHS Management Software — incident reporting, contractor safety management, OSHA recordkeeping, and corrective-action tracking
contextual_vendors.coverage_note: Covers MC-PPL-008, MC-PPL-019, MC-REG-004, MC-REG-016: automated OSHA 300/301 logs, contractor pre-qualification, and 8-hour fatality notification workflows.

control_objectives.statement: Ensure the physical safety of all workers — including contracted labour — at SpaceX Starbase, where OSHA-documented injury rates are nearly 6x the industry average and a fatality on 15 May 2026 has triggered an active investigation.

Manage OSHA regulatory exposure at Starbase arising from the Bautista fatality investigation and the pre-existing contested $115,850 seven-citation penalty package, to prevent escalation to willful or repeat classification.

Ensure contractor and subcontractor workforce at Starbase — including the unnamed scaffold contractor employing Bautista — meets SpaceX's safety and compliance obligations under applicable OSHA construction standards.

Protect SpaceX's reputational standing with NASA, DoD, and the public given media coverage of the 15 May 2026 fatality, SpaceX's no-comment posture, and the pattern of prior Starbase incidents since 2021.

Maintain physical workplace safety controls — specifically fall protection and scaffolding inspection regimes — at Starbase construction sites to prevent recurrence following the 15 May 2026 scaffolding fatality.

master_controls.statement: SpaceX must conduct an immediate documented fall-protection audit across all active Starbase scaffolding work, referencing the 15 May 2026 Bautista fatality and OSHA 29 CFR 1926.451(g) compliance status, before resuming elevated construction activities.

SpaceX must verify that the scaffold contractor employing Bautista had current OSHA-compliant scaffolding training records under 29 CFR 1926.454(a) and must suspend any contractor lacking documented competent-person designation at Starbase.

SpaceX must extend its contractor safety pre-qualification standard to require OSHA injury-rate disclosure and prior-citation history, given the Starbase pattern of subcontractor incidents including the Bautista fatality and the December 2025 CCC Group crane lawsuit.

SpaceX must establish a named safety liaison accountable for contractor worker welfare at Starbase, given city officials' 15 May 2026 statement that 'SpaceX operations are not within the City's purview' left the deceased worker's family with no institutional point of contact.

SpaceX must respond to OSHA Region VI's open rapid-response information request arising from the Bautista fatality before the agency escalates to an on-site inspection, citing the January 2026 precedent where OSHA waited for SpaceX's response on the crane incident.

SpaceX must assess whether any aspect of the Bautista fatality — including the timing relative to the Starship V3 launch and SpaceX's no-comment posture — triggers OSHA's Section 11(c) retaliation-risk review or the agency's programmed inspection criteria for repeat violators.

SpaceX must review the contested $115,850 January 2026 OSHA citation settlement posture in light of the Bautista fatality, because continued contestation combined with a new fatality investigation materially increases the risk of OSHA classifying Starbase violations as 'willful' under 29 CFR 1903.

SpaceX must determine whether the 15 May 2026 fatality required immediate OSHA notification within eight hours under 29 CFR 1904.39, given the prior settled citation for failing to report a hospitalisation — a directly analogous prior failure at Starbase.

SpaceX must audit all active Starbase third-party contractors for valid competent-person designations and scaffold inspection logs, identifying whether the unnamed contractor employing Bautista maintained shift-by-shift inspection records required under 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(3).

SpaceX must insert contractual stop-work authority and OSHA-citation indemnification provisions into all Starbase scaffold and temporary-works subcontracts, given the Bautista fatality and the December 2025 CCC Group lawsuit exposure arising from the same construction programme.

SpaceX must prepare a proactive stakeholder communication addressing the Bautista fatality for NASA and DoD contracting officers, given the acting NASA administrator's December 2025 public criticism of SpaceX and the risk that no-comment silence amplifies contractor-fitness concerns.

SpaceX must verify that personal fall arrest systems and guardrails were installed and inspected at the specific Starbase scaffold from which Bautista fell on 15 May 2026, producing documented evidence for OSHA Region VI's investigation under 29 CFR 1926.451(g)(1).

SpaceX must implement a Starbase-wide scaffolding stand-down inspection, modelled on OSHA's National Safety Stand-Down protocol, covering all scaffolds erected for the Gigabay factory construction currently scheduled for completion by end of 2026.

SpaceX must assess whether the accelerated Gigabay construction timeline — targeting completion by end of 2026 at a $250 million, 700,000-square-foot scale — is creating schedule pressure that is systematically degrading scaffold safety oversight for contracted workers at Starbase.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must conduct an immediate documented fall-protection audit
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's Starbase construction safety director must, before any elevated work resumes on any active scaffold at the Brownsville Starbase facility, complete and sign a documented fall-protection audit of every scaffold in use, explicitly cross-referencing OSHA 29 CFR 1926.451(g) compliance status at each location and citing the 15 May 2026 Bautista fatality as the triggering event. The audit must be completed within 24 hours of this control's issuance, produce a named per-scaffold compliance/non-compliance determination, and be transmitted to SpaceX's legal and EHS leadership simultaneously so that any non-compliant scaffold is formally taken out of service before workers are permitted to return. Evidence of completion is the dated, signed audit log with per-scaffold disposition records retained for OSHA Region VI on request.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control sets the general obligation for a fall-protection audit; this adaptation specifies the 24-hour pre-resumption deadline, the per-scaffold disposition requirement, the dual transmission to legal and EHS given the open OSHA investigation, and the direct evidentiary linkage to the Bautista fatality that the generic control does not supply.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must verify that the scaffold contractor employing
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's contractor management function must, within 48 hours, obtain and verify that the contractor employing Jose Luis Bautista at Starbase on 15 May 2026 held current OSHA-compliant scaffolding training records under 29 CFR 1926.454(a) and had a named, documented competent-person designation on file for the specific scaffold from which Bautista fell. Any contractor — including the Bautista employer — that cannot produce a competent-person designation covering that scaffold on that date must be issued a written suspension from all elevated work at Starbase effective immediately, and that suspension must be communicated in writing to OSHA Region VI as part of SpaceX's investigative cooperation posture. Verification evidence is the contractor's 29 CFR 1926.454(a) training records and the signed competent-person designation document, both dated prior to 15 May 2026.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control requires verification and suspension authority; this adaptation anchors the obligation to the specific scaffold, the specific date, and the specific contractor, and adds the requirement to disclose the suspension outcome to OSHA Region VI given the active investigation — specificity the generic control omits.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must extend its contractor safety pre-qualification standard
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's Starbase procurement and EHS functions must, within 30 days, revise the contractor pre-qualification standard applicable to all Starbase construction subcontracts — including those supporting the Gigabay factory build — to require prospective and incumbent contractors to disclose their three-year OSHA injury-rate history, all open or settled OSHA citations, and any active workplace-fatality or serious-injury litigation as a condition of contract award or renewal. The revised standard must be applied retroactively to all currently active Starbase scaffold and temporary-works contractors, including the contractor that employed Bautista, with any contractor unable or unwilling to provide full disclosure placed on a probationary hold pending review. Evidence of completion is the revised pre-qualification standard document, a dated compliance attestation from each incumbent contractor, and a matrix showing disposition for every active Starbase construction subcontractor.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control calls for extending pre-qualification requirements; this adaptation specifies the three-year lookback window, the retroactive application to incumbents including the Bautista employer, the Gigabay construction programme as a named scope driver, and the probationary-hold mechanism — operational specificity absent from the generic control.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must establish a named safety liaison accountable
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX must, within five business days, designate a named individual — at minimum a senior EHS manager with written delegation of authority — as the Starbase Contractor Worker Safety Liaison, with publicly documented contact information made available to contracted workers, their families, and responding agencies at the Starbase facility gate. This role must have explicit written authority to act as the institutional point of contact for fatality and serious-injury notifications, to liaise directly with OSHA Region VI during the Bautista investigation, and to receive and escalate worker welfare concerns from all contractor workforces active at Starbase, filling the governance vacuum made evident when Starbase city officials stated on 15 May 2026 that SpaceX operations were outside the city's purview and the Bautista family had no institutional contact. Evidence of completion is the signed delegation letter, the posted contact information at the Starbase facility, and written notification to OSHA Region VI of the designated liaison's name and contact details.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control establishes the accountability structure; this adaptation names the specific governance gap — the 15 May 2026 city-official deferral that left the Bautista family without a contact — and adds the OSHA Region VI notification requirement and facility-gate posting obligation, converting an abstract role into an operationally visible one.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must respond to OSHA Region VI's open
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's legal and regulatory affairs function must, within the OSHA-prescribed response window following the 18 May 2026 Region VI investigation confirmation, provide a substantive written response to any OSHA Region VI information request arising from the Bautista fatality, including the scaffold location, the contractor's identity, fall-protection equipment status, and all shift inspection logs for the relevant scaffold — and must not allow the response window to lapse without a written communication to the investigator, given that OSHA's January 2026 crane-incident investigation proceeded to citation while SpaceX's response posture remained passive. The response package must be reviewed by outside counsel before submission, and SpaceX's General Counsel must sign off on the final submission. Evidence of completion is the dated OSHA acknowledgement of SpaceX's response and the internal sign-off record.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control addresses proactive OSHA response; this adaptation specifies the precise content required (scaffold location, contractor identity, fall-protection status, inspection logs), names the January 2026 crane-incident precedent as the risk comparator, and adds the General Counsel sign-off requirement to elevate accountability above the operational level.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must assess whether any aspect of the
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's legal function must, within 10 business days of the 18 May 2026 OSHA investigation confirmation, conduct a documented internal assessment evaluating whether OSHA Region VI is likely to apply its programmed-inspection criteria for repeat violators — given the reported Starbase injury rate nearly six times the industry average for comparable space-vehicle manufacturing facilities and the 17 serious citations reported since 2021 — and whether SpaceX's no-comment public posture on the Bautista fatality could be interpreted as a chilling signal to contracted workers that would trigger OSHA Section 11(c) retaliation-risk scrutiny. If the assessment identifies either risk as plausible, SpaceX must implement a written corrective communication to all Starbase contracted workers affirming their right to report safety concerns to OSHA without fear of retaliation, in advance of any OSHA on-site inspection. Evidence of completion is the dated legal assessment memorandum and, where applicable, the distributed worker communication with distribution confirmation.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control flags the Section 11(c) and repeat-violator inspection risks; this adaptation builds an executable assessment protocol with a defined output condition (corrective worker communication) triggered by specific risk findings, and names the no-comment posture and reported injury-rate data as the concrete factors the assessor must evaluate.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must review the contested $115
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's legal function must, within 15 days of the 18 May 2026 OSHA investigation confirmation, prepare a written strategic reassessment of the decision to contest the January 2026 OSHA citations totalling $115,850 — which included seven serious violations related to the crane collapse — in light of the Bautista fatality, explicitly modelling the probability that OSHA reclassifies one or more of the contested violations from 'serious' to 'willful' under 29 CFR 1903 given a concurrent fatality investigation on the same Starbase site. The reassessment must be presented to SpaceX's General Counsel and Chief Financial Officer with a recommendation on whether to continue contesting, negotiate a settlement, or withdraw the contest, and must include a quantified exposure estimate for willful-classification penalties. Evidence of completion is the dated legal memorandum delivered to General Counsel and CFO with a documented disposition decision recorded within five business days of delivery.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control identifies the willful-reclassification risk; this adaptation operationalises it by requiring a quantified exposure estimate, naming the specific contested citation amount and violation type, setting a decision deadline, and requiring CFO involvement to ensure the financial exposure is evaluated at an appropriate authority level.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must determine whether the 15 May 2026
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's EHS and legal functions must immediately — and no later than by end of business on the day this control is issued — produce a documented determination of whether the 15 May 2026 Bautista fatality was reported to OSHA Region VI within eight hours as required under 29 CFR 1904.39(a)(2), with particular attention to the fact that Bautista was pronounced dead at 5:15 a.m. at Valley Regional Medical Center, making the eight-hour reporting clock calculable with precision. If the determination reveals that the notification was late or absent, SpaceX must immediately make a voluntary late notification to OSHA Region VI in writing and must document the reason for the reporting delay — this action is non-discretionary given SpaceX's prior settled citation for failure to report an in-patient hospitalisation, which makes a second reporting-timeliness failure an aggravated recurrence. Evidence of completion is the internal determination memo with the precise notification time recorded, and, if applicable, the written late-notification submission to OSHA Region VI with a delivery confirmation.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control identifies the 29 CFR 1904.39 notification risk; this adaptation converts it into an immediate, time-bound verification obligation, uses the known 5:15 a.m. death pronouncement to make the eight-hour clock explicit and calculable, and establishes the voluntary late-notification path as a non-discretionary consequence of the prior settled citation — specificity the generic control cannot provide.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must audit all active Starbase third-party contractors
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's contractor management and EHS functions must, within 72 hours of this control's issuance, audit all third-party contractors currently performing scaffolding or elevated construction work at Starbase to verify that each holds a current, named competent-person designation and that shift-by-shift scaffold inspection logs required under 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(3) exist and are up to date for all active scaffolds. For the specific contractor that employed Bautista, SpaceX must obtain or reconstruct the inspection log for the scaffold from which he fell on 15 May 2026 — covering at minimum the shift immediately preceding the fatality — and must retain that log in a secured evidentiary file dedicated to the OSHA Region VI investigation. Any contractor unable to produce compliant inspection logs must be suspended from elevated work at Starbase pending remediation. Evidence of completion is the completed audit matrix listing every active scaffold contractor, competent-person name and designation date, and inspection-log status, countersigned by SpaceX's Starbase EHS lead.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control requires a competent-person and inspection-log audit; this adaptation specifies the 72-hour window, isolates the Bautista contractor's pre-fatality shift log as a discrete evidentiary preservation obligation for the OSHA investigation, and adds the suspension consequence — details that convert the generic audit into an incident-responsive executable directive.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must insert contractual stop-work authority and OSHA-citation
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's legal and procurement functions must, within 30 days, insert into every Starbase scaffold and temporary-works subcontract — including all contracts supporting the Gigabay factory construction — explicit contractual provisions granting SpaceX unilateral stop-work authority for safety non-compliance, requiring the subcontractor to indemnify SpaceX for OSHA citation penalties directly attributable to the subcontractor's acts or omissions, and mandating immediate written notice to SpaceX of any OSHA contact or worker injury. These provisions must be applied to all currently active Starbase subcontracts via executed contract amendments, not merely to future awards, given that the contractor employing Bautista was operating under existing contractual arrangements that did not prevent the 15 May 2026 fatality. Evidence of completion is the fully executed amendment for every active Starbase scaffold and temporary-works subcontract, with a compliance matrix listing contractor name, amendment execution date, and contract number.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control calls for contractual stop-work and indemnification provisions; this adaptation requires retroactive amendment of all active subcontracts — not just future awards — specifies the OSHA-contact notification obligation as a distinct provision, and anchors the urgency in the fact that existing contracts failed to prevent the Bautista fatality.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must prepare a proactive stakeholder communication addressing
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's government affairs and communications functions must, within five business days, prepare and deliver a written briefing to the relevant NASA contracting officers and DoD programme managers covering: the confirmed facts of the 15 May 2026 Bautista fatality, SpaceX's immediate safety actions taken in response, the status of the OSHA Region VI investigation, and SpaceX's corrective commitments with named accountable owners and target dates. The briefing must be delivered proactively — not in response to a contracting officer inquiry — given that SpaceX's public no-comment posture means these stakeholders are currently receiving information exclusively through media reporting, and given the acting NASA administrator's December 2025 public criticisms of SpaceX contractor fitness that make silence on a worker fatality a material programme-relationship risk. Evidence of completion is the dated transmission records to each named contracting officer and programme manager, and the briefing document itself.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control identifies the stakeholder communication risk; this adaptation specifies the briefing's required content elements, names the NASA administrator's December 2025 criticism as the pre-existing relationship risk that makes proactive outreach non-optional, and requires proactive delivery rather than reactive response — distinguishing it from a generic communications obligation.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must verify that personal fall arrest systems
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's EHS function must, in coordination with the OSHA Region VI investigation, produce documented verification — within 48 hours or by the time OSHA requests physical evidence, whichever is earlier — of whether personal fall arrest systems (PFAS) and guardrails meeting the specifications of 29 CFR 1926.451(g)(1) were installed, inspected, and in serviceable condition at the specific Starbase scaffold from which Jose Luis Bautista fell at approximately 5:00–5:15 a.m. on 15 May 2026. This verification must include photographic or video evidence of the scaffold's current state, the pre-incident inspection log for that scaffold, and any PFAS equipment recovered at the scene, all of which must be preserved in a secured evidentiary chain of custody; SpaceX must not alter, remove, or repair the scaffold or surrounding area without written OSHA Region VI authorisation. Evidence of completion is the signed chain-of-custody log, the evidentiary file delivered to legal counsel, and written OSHA Region VI acknowledgement of SpaceX's preservation notification.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control requires verification of fall-protection equipment; this adaptation adds a site-preservation obligation and chain-of-custody requirement reflecting the live OSHA investigation, specifies the exact regulatory standard against which the scaffold must be evaluated, and names the precise time window of the fatality to anchor the verification to the specific physical state of the scaffold at the time of incident.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must implement a Starbase-wide scaffolding stand-down inspection
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's Starbase construction safety director must, within 72 hours of this control's issuance, initiate a formal scaffolding safety stand-down across all Starbase construction sites — encompassing all scaffolds erected in support of the Gigabay factory build and any other concurrent construction activity — modelled on OSHA's National Safety Stand-Down protocol, during which no elevated scaffold work may proceed. The stand-down must include a toolbox safety briefing for all contracted workers on fall-protection requirements under 29 CFR 1926.451(g), an acknowledgement sign-off from each worker and their supervisor, and a per-scaffold inspection by a named competent person before that scaffold is returned to service. The stand-down completion package — including attendance records, per-scaffold return-to-service sign-offs, and the competent person's name for each inspection — must be filed with SpaceX's legal team for retention as part of the OSHA Region VI investigation record. Evidence of completion is the dated stand-down completion package countersigned by the Starbase construction safety director.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control calls for a stand-down inspection; this adaptation scopes it explicitly to the Gigabay construction programme, requires individual worker acknowledgement sign-offs that create an evidentiary record for the OSHA investigation, and mandates legal-team retention of the completion package — converting a safety protocol into a simultaneous regulatory risk-management instrument.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: SpaceX must assess whether the accelerated Gigabay construction
adaptive_controls.statement: SpaceX's Starbase programme management and EHS functions must, within 21 days, commission and complete a formal written schedule-pressure safety assessment evaluating whether the Gigabay factory construction timeline — targeting completion by end of 2026 across a reported 700,000-square-foot, $250 million build — is systematically reducing the time available for scaffold inspection, competent-person oversight, and contractor safety compliance at Starbase, and whether schedule-driven acceleration contributed to the conditions that resulted in the 15 May 2026 Bautista fatality. The assessment must be conducted by an independent qualified safety professional not embedded in the Gigabay programme team, must quantify the ratio of scaffold-competent-person hours to total scaffold-worker-hours over the preceding 90 days, and must produce a named set of schedule or resourcing adjustments if the ratio falls below the programme's own stated safety standards. The completed assessment must be reviewed and signed by SpaceX's Chief Safety Officer or equivalent, and a copy must be made available to OSHA Region VI on request. Evidence of completion is the dated assessment report with the named assessor's credentials, the Chief Safety Officer signature, and the disposition record for each recommended adjustment.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The master control identifies the schedule-pressure risk; this adaptation operationalises it by requiring an independent assessor outside the Gigabay programme team, specifying a quantitative competent-person-to-worker-hour ratio as the measurable indicator of resourcing adequacy, and explicitly connecting the schedule assessment back to the Bautista fatality as the investigative lens — specificity unavailable in the generic control.

risk_coverage_vendors.name: SafetyCulture
risk_coverage_vendors.product_name: SafetyCulture – OSHA Scaffold Inspection Checklist / Construction Safety Platform
risk_coverage_vendors.what_they_do: SafetyCulture (formerly iAuditor) is a mobile-first operations and safety platform that provides digital checklists, inspection workflows, real-time hazard reporting, and training delivery tools for construction and industrial worksites, including purpose-built OSHA-compliant scaffold inspection templates.
risk_coverage_vendors.fit_rationale: SafetyCulture's documented OSHA Scaffold Inspection Checklist is explicitly designed to inspect scaffolds and scaffold parts before each work shift and after any event that may have caused damage — directly matching the pre-shift competent-person inspection requirement in 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(3) that is the subject of MC-PPL-008, MC-PHY-009, and MC-PHY-010. The platform also covers guardrail and fall arrest system verification required by MC-PHY-009. However, SafetyCulture is a software tool; it does not itself conduct physical scaffold inspections or produce a legally signed audit by a named individual — the human competent person must still be designated by the client.

capability_claims.claim: SafetyCulture's OSHA Scaffold Inspection Checklist is designed to inspect scaffolds and scaffold parts before each work shift, and after any event that may have caused damage.
capability_claims.source_span: Use this OSHA Scaffold Inspection Checklist to inspect scaffolds and scaffold parts before each work shift, and after any event that may have caused damage.

capability_claims.claim: SafetyCulture's scaffold checklist verifies guardrails and mid-rails and ensures falling object protection, and captures findings, photos, and signatures to generate a compliant report while on-site.
capability_claims.source_span: confirm guardrails and mid-rails, and ensure falling object protection. Check tie-ins, braces, and guying per manufacturer guidance. Capture findings, photos, and signatures to generate a compliant report with SafetyCulture.

capability_claims.claim: SafetyCulture documents that OSHA requires a visual inspection to be conducted by a competent person before the start of every shift and after conducting work, and provides scaffold checklists to support this requirement.
capability_claims.source_span: It is required that a visual inspection be conducted by a competent person before the start of every shift and after conducting work.

capability_claims.claim: SafetyCulture provides training modules covering fall protection requirements under OSHA 29 CFR 1926.451(g), including guardrail installation, personal fall arrest systems (PFAS), and proper use of PPE.
capability_claims.source_span: Falls consistently rank as a leading cause of workplace fatalities. As such, courses involving fall protection ( OSHA #3115 ) are non-negotiable in construction scaffold safety training.

mitigation_mechanism.summary: SafetyCulture provides a pre-configured OSHA Scaffold Inspection Checklist that digitises the shift-by-shift inspection regime required by 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(3). Inspectors use the mobile platform to check guardrails, mid-rails, personal fall arrest systems, footings, baseplates, braces, and planking against OSHA-compliant criteria, capturing photos and inspector signatures in a tamper-evident digital log. For MC-PHY-009, the platform preserves a per-scaffold evidentiary record (photos, inspection findings, signatures) that can be retained for OSHA Region VI on request. For MC-PHY-010, SafetyCulture supports a scaffolding stand-down by enabling mass checklist deployment to all active scaffolds simultaneously, with return-to-service sign-offs tracked per scaffold. For MC-PPL-008, the platform's pre-shift inspection workflow ensures no scaffold is returned to service after the 15 May 2026 fatality without a documented competent-person review. The client must separately designate the named competent person.
grounding_spans: Use this OSHA Scaffold Inspection Checklist to inspect scaffolds and scaffold parts before each work shift, and after any event that may have caused damage.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Provides an OSHA-compliant scaffold inspection checklist for use before each work shift and after any damaging event, covering all MC-PPL-008 required inspection points.
enabling_features.source_span: Use this OSHA Scaffold Inspection Checklist to inspect scaffolds and scaffold parts before each work shift, and after any event that may have caused damage.

enabling_features.feature: Digital Evidentiary Log with Photos and Signatures
enabling_features.what_it_does: Captures inspector signatures, photos, and per-scaffold findings in a digital log that supports the chain-of-custody record required for OSHA Region VI's investigation under MC-PHY-009.
enabling_features.source_span: Capture findings, photos, and signatures to generate a compliant report with SafetyCulture.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Provides bite-sized training modules covering OSHA fall protection, guardrail installation, PFAS use, and competent-person inspection procedures, supporting the worker-readiness component of MC-PPL-019.
enabling_features.source_span: Create and share bite-sized modules on general construction safety guidelines, load limits, and fall protection. Empower workers who will erect, use, inspect, and dismantle these temporary platforms by enabling instant safety alerts, tracking real-time hazards, and assigning corrective actions.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Pre-shift shift-by-shift scaffold inspection workflow per 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(3)
dimension_coverage.note: SafetyCulture's OSHA Scaffold Inspection Checklist is explicitly designed for before-each-shift use.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Use this OSHA Scaffold Inspection Checklist to inspect scaffolds and scaffold parts before each work shift, and after any event that may have caused damage.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Named competent-person designation per 29 CFR 1926.454(a)
dimension_coverage.note: SafetyCulture captures who performed the inspection but does not certify or designate the individual as a competent person — that designation is a human HR/EHS action the client must take independently.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Make sure that only trained and fully equipped personnel will be performing the task.

risk_coverage_vendors.score_rationale: SafetyCulture maps precisely to the scaffold inspection and fall protection controls at issue (D1/D3 high) but evidence rests on product-page and template claims rather than named customer case studies or independent validation, and its strong brand recognition introduces a moderate reputation-halo factor that limits the independence score.

risk_coverage_vendors.name: Cority
risk_coverage_vendors.product_name: Cority Contractor Management Software
risk_coverage_vendors.what_they_do: Cority provides a construction EHS+ platform with a dedicated contractor management module that manages the full contractor lifecycle — from pre-qualification to ongoing monitoring and post-job review — including training verification, competent-person qualification tracking, and permit-to-work integration.
risk_coverage_vendors.fit_rationale: Cority's contractor management module is documented as managing qualifications, permits, training, and performance in one system, including pre-qualification of contractors with safety performance tracking — directly addressing the MC-PPL-020 requirement to extend SpaceX's contractor pre-qualification standard to require OSHA injury-rate disclosure and the MC-PPL-019/MC-TPR-021 requirement to verify competent-person designations and training records. The platform also supports permit-to-work for high-risk tasks and stop-work authority (MC-TPR-022). However, contractual indemnification provisions must be drafted by legal counsel.

capability_claims.claim: Cority contractor management software manages qualifications, permits, training, and performance in one system, automating pre-qualification to ensure contractors are qualified and covered to perform work on site.
capability_claims.source_span: Manage qualifications, permits, training, and performance in one system.

capability_claims.claim: Cority automates pre-qualification to ensure contractors are qualified and covered to perform work on site, giving 360-degree visibility into contractor risk to identify hazards early and reduce exposure.
capability_claims.source_span: Automate prequalification to ensure contractors are qualified, and covered to perform work on site. Gain 360° visibility into contractor risk to identify hazards early and reduce exposure.

capability_claims.claim: Cority's SIF Prevention feature identifies high-risk contractors and prioritizes safety interventions, and its AI-powered insights flag contractor risk patterns and concerns early.
capability_claims.source_span: Identify high-risk contractors and prioritize safety interventions. Embedded AI surfaces contractor risk patterns and flags concerns early.

capability_claims.claim: Cority's construction EHS software ensures only trained, qualified and competent contractors and subcontractors can access and work on construction projects.
capability_claims.source_span: Ensuring only trained, qualified and competent contractors and subcontractors can access and work on construction projects.

mitigation_mechanism.summary: Cority's Contractor Management module addresses the core preventive requirements arising from the Bautista fatality. For MC-PPL-020, the platform's pre-qualification workflow can be configured to require all Starbase scaffold contractors to disclose OSHA injury-rate history and citation records as a gate to site access. For MC-PPL-019 and MC-TPR-021, Cority tracks training records and competent-person designations at the individual worker level, enabling SpaceX to verify that each scaffold-erector holds current OSHA 29 CFR 1926.454(a) training before any elevated work begins. For MC-TPR-022, the permit-to-work module enforces approval workflows before high-risk tasks commence, embedding a de facto stop-work authority gate. Cority's AI-powered risk scoring identifies contractor risk patterns early, supporting proactive exclusion of high-risk contractors from site access.
grounding_spans: Automate prequalification to ensure contractors are qualified, and covered to perform work on site.
Identify high-risk contractors and prioritize safety interventions.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Automates pre-qualification including OSHA injury-rate, training, and certification verification, ensuring only qualified contractors gain site access — supporting MC-PPL-020's requirement to extend SpaceX's contractor pre-qualification standard.
enabling_features.source_span: Automate prequalification to ensure contractors are qualified, and covered to perform work on site.

enabling_features.feature: SIF Prevention and AI-Powered Contractor Risk Scoring
enabling_features.what_it_does: Identifies high-risk contractors and prioritises safety interventions before incidents occur, supporting MC-TPR-021's requirement to audit all active Starbase contractors for safety compliance.
enabling_features.source_span: SIF Prevention. Identify high-risk contractors and prioritize safety interventions. AI-Powered Insights. Embedded AI surfaces contractor risk patterns and flags concerns early.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Enables contractors to complete risk assessments, inspections, and close actions from the field on mobile devices, supporting MC-TPR-021's shift-by-shift scaffold inspection log requirement.
enabling_features.source_span: Mobile-Enabled Field Management. Contractors complete risk assessments, inspections, and close actions anywhere.

dimension_coverage.dimension: OSHA injury-rate and citation history disclosure as pre-qualification gate
dimension_coverage.note: Cority's pre-qualification workflow automates the collection of safety performance data as a condition of site access.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Automate prequalification to ensure contractors are qualified, and covered to perform work on site.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Shift-by-shift scaffold inspection logs per 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(3)
dimension_coverage.note: Cority's mobile field management supports inspection log capture, but the inspection workflow must be configured by the client to match the specific 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(3) shift-inspection format.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Contractors complete risk assessments, inspections, and close actions anywhere.

risk_coverage_vendors.score_rationale: Cority's dedicated construction EHS contractor management module aligns closely with the MCs (pre-qualification, training verification, permit-to-work, competent-person tracking) and the scaffolding-fall scenario's root causes, but all verified claims derive from marketing/product-page copy with no named customer case studies or third-party certifications, capping evidence strength and keeping the score in the inferred tier.

risk_coverage_vendors.name: ISN (ISNetworld)
risk_coverage_vendors.product_name: ISNetworld – Contractor Prequalification & Ongoing Monitoring
risk_coverage_vendors.what_they_do: ISN is the global leader in contractor and supplier information management, operating ISNetworld — a platform that enables hiring clients to collect, verify, trend, and audit contractors' health and safety programs, OSHA injury statistics, training records, and insurance documentation as a condition of site access.
risk_coverage_vendors.fit_rationale: ISNetworld directly documents the capability to streamline contractor pre-qualification by requiring contractors to submit health, safety, sustainability, and OSHA-injury-rate information, and to assign and track worker-level training and qualifications — both directly addressing MC-PPL-020 and MC-TPR-021. The platform also allows hiring clients to access verified third-party data including OSHA logs. However, ISNetworld is a registry/platform, not a physical audit service; the client must separately designate the competent person for each scaffold.

capability_claims.claim: ISNetworld streamlines the prequalification process and ongoing monitoring by enabling contractors to submit health, safety, sustainability, and procurement information for multiple Hiring Clients within one subscription.
capability_claims.source_span: Contractor Prequalification & Ongoing Monitoring. Streamline the prequalification process and ongoing monitoring by enabling contractors to submit health, safety, sustainability,

capability_claims.claim: ISNetworld requires contractors to develop, implement and maintain written health and safety programs and document past and present injury statistics, giving hiring clients a clear picture of contractors' safe work practices including OSHA logs.
capability_claims.source_span: Contractors are required to develop, implement and maintain written health and safety programs as well as document past and present injury statistics.

mitigation_mechanism.summary: ISNetworld addresses the MC-PPL-020 requirement to extend SpaceX's contractor pre-qualification standard by providing a structured digital registry where all Starbase scaffold and temporary-works contractors must submit their OSHA injury-rate history (TRIR, DART), open or contested citations, and written health and safety programs as a condition of receiving a qualified status and site access. Hiring clients can set custom thresholds — e.g., suspending access for contractors with TRIR above a defined rate or with open fatality-related citations. For MC-TPR-021, ISNetworld's worker-level training module enables SpaceX to assign and track OSHA 29 CFR 1926.454(a) scaffolding training records at the individual worker level, providing documentary evidence that each scaffold worker is qualified before they access any Starbase scaffold.
grounding_spans: Streamline the prequalification process and ongoing monitoring by enabling contractors to submit health, safety, sustainability, and procurement information for multiple Hiring Clients within one subscription.

Worker-Level Training & Qualifications. Assign and track completion of contractor and supplier worker-level trainings and qualifications required to perform specified tasks to meet Hiring Client and regulatory requirements.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Requires all Starbase scaffold contractors to submit OSHA injury-rate history, citation records, and health and safety programs as a pre-qualification gate, directly supporting MC-PPL-020.
enabling_features.source_span: Contractor Prequalification & Ongoing Monitoring. Streamline the prequalification process and ongoing monitoring by enabling contractors to submit health, safety, sustainability, and procurement information for multiple Hiring Clients within one subscription.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Verified OSHA citation history for incumbent contractors
dimension_coverage.note: ISNetworld provides direct access to third-party OSHA data within the platform.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Access verified records directly in ISNetworld from numerous third parties to help ensure compliance with regulatory and Hiring Client requirements.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Competent-person designation at individual scaffold level
dimension_coverage.note: ISNetworld tracks worker qualifications and training completion, but the competent-person designation for a specific scaffold on a specific shift is a physical/HR action by SpaceX's EHS team — not a platform capability.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Worker-Level Training & Qualifications. Assign and track completion of contractor and supplier worker-level trainings and qualifications required to perform specified tasks to meet Hiring Client and regulatory requirements.

mitigation_limits.limit: ISNetworld does not conduct physical site audits, designate competent persons, or produce the legal documentation for contractual stop-work authority provisions — those require SpaceX's legal and EHS functions acting independently.
grounding_spans: Streamline the prequalification process and ongoing monitoring by enabling contractors to submit health, safety, sustainability, and procurement information for multiple Hiring Clients within one subscription.

risk_coverage_vendors.score_rationale: ISN's core product directly addresses third-party contractor safety prequalification and OSHA recordkeeping (MC-PPL-020, MC-TPR-021), giving solid coverage specificity for this scaffolding fatality scenario, but evidence is limited to product-page claims with no named customer case studies, and ISN's dominant market position introduces meaningful reputation-halo risk that tempers the independence score.

incident_handler_vendors.name: Fisher Phillips LLP
incident_handler_vendors.product_name: Workplace Safety and Catastrophe Management Practice Group
incident_handler_vendors.what_they_do: Fisher Phillips' Workplace Safety and Catastrophe Management Practice Group provides OSHA defense counsel, catastrophe response, OSHA investigation representation, and safety compliance advisory for employers across all industries including construction, with a documented practice of responding immediately following workplace fatalities and defending willful, egregious, and repeat OSHA citations.
incident_handler_vendors.handles_scenario: Fisher Phillips responds to OSHA fatality investigations, defends employers against willful and repeat OSHA citations, manages catastrophe response including media relations and family communications, and conducts immediate post-accident investigations to protect legal privilege — directly matching the incident scenario of a worker fatality under OSHA Region VI investigation at Starbase with pre-existing contested citations.
incident_handler_vendors.fit_rationale: Fisher Phillips' practice page explicitly documents: (1) responding immediately after an accident occurs including conducting investigations before an OSHA inspector arrives; (2) defending cases involving repeat or willful citations; (3) catastrophe management including media relations, family communications, and preparation for civil or criminal proceedings; and (4) safety consulting including site safety audits. All four capabilities directly address the MC-REG-004, MC-REG-008, MC-REG-014, and MC-REP-016 incident-response requirements.

capability_claims.claim: Fisher Phillips' team gets involved at the most critical stage: conducting investigations immediately after an accident has occurred, because when an OSHA inspector arrives, prior probes can open the door to additional claims such as workers compensation or wrongful death.

mitigation_mechanism.summary: Following the 18 May 2026 OSHA Region VI investigation confirmation, Fisher Phillips' Workplace Safety and Catastrophe Management team would be engaged to: (1) immediately conduct a privilege-protected post-accident investigation before OSHA arrives for an on-site inspection, documenting the scaffold's state, PFAS records, and competent-person designation status (MC-REG-004, MC-PHY-009); (2) respond to OSHA Region VI's information requests and manage the inspection posture to avoid escalation to willful classification, drawing on the firm's documented repeat and willful citation defence experience (MC-REG-008, MC-REG-014); (3) conduct a strategic reassessment of whether to continue contesting the January 2026 $115,850 citation package in light of the new fatality (MC-REG-014); (4) manage catastrophe communications including media, family communications, and government stakeholder briefings (MC-REP-016); and (5) conduct a site safety audit to identify and document corrective actions across all active Starbase scaffolds before OSHA's on-site inspection.

incident.summary: Following California Dairies Inc.'s 20 April 2026 voluntary recall of bulk Low Heat Non-Fat Dried Milk Powder and Buttermilk Powder over potential Salmonella contamination, the downstream cascade continued to grow through the 22–23 May 2026 window, with the FDA's dedicated major-recalls tracking page updated multiple times and the compiled product list reaching approximately two dozen distinct SKUs as of 20 May. California Dairies supplies an estimated 40% of the US dried milk powder market, meaning the recall affected a wide range of snack foods, seasonings, frozen pizzas, and meat-and-poultry products sold at Target, Walmart, Aldi, and other major retailers nationwide. No illnesses have been confirmed, but the FDA stated it was continuing to identify additional downstream products, keeping supply chain disruption risk open-ended.
incident.severity_rationale: A supplier holding ~40% US market share for a common food ingredient triggered a cascading multi-agency recall affecting approximately two dozen distinct consumer product lines sold nationwide, with the investigation still open-ended at the time of disclosure.

secondary_mappings.why: A single bulk-ingredient supplier's contamination event propagated through multiple tiers of the US food manufacturing supply chain, triggering recalls at dozens of downstream consumer brands.
FDA and USDA FSIS both issued formal recall notices and public health alerts, with active multi-agency investigation and ongoing downstream product identification.
Broad national media coverage of named downstream brands (Ghirardelli, Zapp's, Fisher, Good & Gather, etc.) created significant reputational exposure across the food sector.
Multiple consumer brands were harmed not by their own direct suppliers but by a shared upstream ingredient supplier two or more tiers deep in their supply networks.
