dimension_coverage.note: Duo's New User Policy can be set to deny OWA access to users who have not enrolled in MFA, satisfying the AC-CYB-017-XSS-01 suspension requirement.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: change the "New user policy" to "Require Enrollment." This will prompt all users to authenticate (or enroll) after they type in their usernames and passwords.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Session-token theft prevention post-MFA
dimension_coverage.note: MFA enforcement limits the impact of CVE-2026-42897 session-token theft (a token can only be stolen from an already-authenticated MFA session), but does not prevent a threat actor from stealing the post-MFA session token itself if the user has already completed MFA and opened a crafted email.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Duo adds two-factor authentication to Outlook Web App (OWA) logins, offering inline user enrollment, self-service device management, and support for a variety of authentication methods

dimension_coverage.dimension: Air-gapped or Duo-offline Exchange nodes
dimension_coverage.note: Duo can be configured to 'fail closed' for offline nodes (blocking all OWA access) or 'fail open' (allowing bypass). Fail-closed provides the most secure posture for air-gapped environments but blocks legitimate user access.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: if the Bypass Duo authentication when offline option is unchecked, then Duo for OWA will "fail closed" when Duo Security cloud services are unreachable and users will not be able to access protected federated resources.

mitigation_limits.limit: Duo for OWA does not prevent session-token theft from an already-authenticated MFA session where a user opens a crafted email; it reduces the attack surface by requiring MFA before session establishment but cannot fully prevent post-login XSS exploitation.
grounding_spans: Duo adds two-factor authentication to Outlook Web App (OWA) logins, offering inline user enrollment, self-service device management, and support for a variety of authentication methods

mitigation_limits.limit: Duo requires outbound TCP port 443 connectivity to Duo's cloud service; in fail-closed mode, air-gapped Exchange nodes cannot issue OWA sessions at all when the Duo service is unreachable.
grounding_spans: This application communicates with Duo's service on SSL TCP port 443.

risk_coverage_vendors.score_rationale: Duo's OWA MFA integration is precisely matched to the affected platform (Exchange 2016/2019/SE) and directly covers the authentication layer, earning high specificity, but the XSS exploit fires before authentication completes — meaning MFA intercepts logins yet cannot neutralize a crafted-email JavaScript payload that executes inside an already-authenticated OWA session, materially limiting incident relevance and dragging D3 down.

risk_coverage_vendors.name: Splunk Inc. (a Cisco company)
risk_coverage_vendors.product_name: Splunk Enterprise Security (ESCU — HAFNIUM Group Analytic Story)
risk_coverage_vendors.what_they_do: Splunk Enterprise Security with the ESCU (Enterprise Security Content Update) HAFNIUM Group Analytic Story provides pre-built detection searches targeting Exchange Server OWA exploitation patterns — including W3WP.exe spawning shells, IIS log patterns indicative of OWA exploit chains, email forwarding rule creation, and post-exploitation credential dumping — directly addressing MC-CYB-019.
risk_coverage_vendors.fit_rationale: Splunk has published dedicated, specifically named Exchange Server OWA zero-day detection content (HAFNIUM Group Analytic Story via ESCU) covering the exact log patterns referenced in MC-CYB-019: IIS log anomalies on OWA URL paths, W3WP.exe spawning command shells indicating OWA exploitation, and email forwarding rule creation post-compromise. The analytic story also includes HttpProxy log ingestion guidance specific to Exchange CVE exploitation patterns.

capability_claims.claim: Splunk's Threat Research team released an Analytic Story called 'HAFNIUM group' via Enterprise Security Content Update version 3.16 to provide visibility into adversary actions exploiting Microsoft Exchange zero-days, including detections for W3WP.exe spawning shells and OWA-specific IIS log patterns.
capability_claims.source_span: the Splunk Threat Research team released an Analytic Story called "HAFNIUM group" via Enterprise Security Content Update version 3.16 on March 8th to provide visibility into these adversary actions.

capability_claims.claim: Splunk provides SPL detection searches targeting W3WP.exe (IIS worker process) spawning command shells — the primary Exchange OWA exploitation indicator — using both Sysmon and Windows Security Event Log sources.
capability_claims.source_span: W3wp.exe Spawning Shell. This query identifies a shell, PowerShell.exe or Cmd.exe, spawning from W3WP.exe, or IIS.

capability_claims.claim: Splunk provides Exchange HttpProxy log ingestion guidance and an SPL search targeting the OABVirtualDirectory reset POST pattern in Exchange IIS logs — the specific log signature of the OWA exploit chain.
capability_claims.source_span: This search is based on the mshttp_proxy sourcetype that we defined earlier. It picks up the reset of the OAB Virtual Directory, which is called out in the Praetorian blog.

framework_alignments.clause: T1505.003 (Server Software Component: Web Shell), T1114.001 (Email Collection: Local Email Collection)
framework_alignments.source_span: T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell W3WP.exe Spawning Shell

mitigation_mechanism.summary: Splunk Enterprise Security with the ESCU HAFNIUM Group Analytic Story ingests Exchange HttpProxy logs, Windows Security Event Logs, and Sysmon telemetry from Exchange servers and applies pre-built detection searches targeting: (a) POST requests to OWA exploit paths in IIS/HttpProxy logs; (b) W3WP.exe spawning command shells (the primary OWA exploitation indicator); (c) ASPX webshell files written to Exchange directories post-exploitation; and (d) post-exploitation credential dumping (LSASS via ProcDump), new account creation, and lateral movement. For MC-CYB-019 specifically, the HttpProxy log ingestion guidance and OWA-path detection searches provide the SOC with the detection rules targeting OWA log patterns indicative of CVE-2026-42897-style JavaScript injection, and the W3WP shell-spawn rules detect post-exploitation execution.
grounding_spans: the Splunk Threat Research team released an Analytic Story called "HAFNIUM group" via Enterprise Security Content Update version 3.16 on March 8th to provide visibility into these adversary actions.

Network data sources, such as firewall, VPN and web application firewall (WAF) can be useful to monitor for communication inbound to the OWA portion of the Exchange server

enabling_features.what_it_does: SPL search identifying PowerShell.exe or Cmd.exe spawning from W3WP.exe (IIS worker process), the primary indicator of OWA exploitation, using both Sysmon (Event Code 1) and Windows Security Event Logs (Event Code 4688).
enabling_features.source_span: W3wp.exe Spawning Shell. This query identifies a shell, PowerShell.exe or Cmd.exe, spawning from W3WP.exe, or IIS.

enabling_features.feature: OWA Auth Directory Webshell Write Detection
enabling_features.what_it_does: Sysmon- and Security-event-based SPL searches detecting ASPX file writes to the Exchange OWA auth directory by the SYSTEM account (S-1-5-18), identifying CVE-2026-42897-style post-exploitation webshell drops.
enabling_features.source_span: file_path="C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*"

workflow_steps.steps:
1. Configure Exchange HttpProxy log ingestion: Deploy the Splunk Universal Forwarder on each Exchange server and configure inputs.conf with the crcSalt directive to ingest Exchange HttpProxy CSV logs as sourcetype ms:exchange:http_proxy.
2. Deploy ESCU HAFNIUM Analytic Story: Install Enterprise Security Content Update version 3.16 or later and activate the HAFNIUM Group Analytic Story to deploy all pre-built Exchange OWA exploit detection searches.
3. Activate W3WP shell spawn detection: Enable the 'W3WP Spawning Shell' SPL searches targeting Sysmon Event Code 1 and Windows Security Event Code 4688 to flag command/PowerShell processes spawning from w3wp.exe.
4. Activate OWA HttpProxy POST anomaly detection: Enable the HttpProxy sourcetype search targeting POST requests containing ResetOABVirtualDirectory to detect OWA exploit chain activity.
5. Configure ASPX webshell file-write alerting: Enable the Sysmon NewScriptWritten search targeting the Exchange OWA auth directory for .aspx file writes by the SYSTEM account.
6. Set alert routing: Configure all triggered alerts to route to the incident response queue with P2 priority, as required by AC-CYB-019-XSS-01, and validate with test-case data before the 2026-05-23 deployment deadline.
grounding_spans: the Splunk Threat Research team released an Analytic Story called "HAFNIUM group" via Enterprise Security Content Update version 3.16 on March 8th to provide visibility into these adversary actions.

dimension_coverage.note: Splunk's research.splunk.com content library includes multiple analytics for email forwarding rule creation detection (O365 New Email Forwarding Rule, O365 Mailbox Email Forwarding Enabled) applicable to Exchange environments with appropriate log sources.

dimension_coverage.note: Splunk supports configurable alert actions and priority routing to SOAR/ticketing systems, but the specific P2 priority queue routing required by AC-CYB-019-XSS-01 must be configured by the customer.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: the Splunk Threat Research team released an Analytic Story called "HAFNIUM group" via Enterprise Security Content Update version 3.16 on March 8th to provide visibility into these adversary actions.

mitigation_limits.limit: Splunk's ESCU HAFNIUM Analytic Story was developed for the 2021 Exchange zero-day family (CVE-2021-26855 et al.); customers must validate that SPL searches apply to CVE-2026-42897 XSS-specific indicators and may need to supplement with custom rules for session cookie reuse anomalies.
grounding_spans: the Splunk Threat Research team released an Analytic Story called "HAFNIUM group" via Enterprise Security Content Update version 3.16 on March 8th to provide visibility into these adversary actions.

mitigation_limits.limit: Splunk does not perform EEMS Health Checker verification, EOMT execution, or mailbox forwarding rule revocation; it is a detection and investigation platform only.
grounding_spans: For post breach identification of lateral movement or exfiltration, network data sources as well as wire data, like Stream for Splunk or Zeek can play a role in understanding north/south and east/west communications.

risk_coverage_vendors.score_rationale: Three verified capability claims demonstrate highly specific SPL detections for OWA/Exchange exploitation patterns (W3WP.exe shell-spawning, IIS proxy log analysis) that map directly to CVE-2026-42897's post-exploitation indicators, but evidence is sourced from Splunk's own threat research blog rather than independent customer case studies, and Splunk's dominant SIEM reputation introduces meaningful selection-bias risk that tempers the independence score.

risk_coverage_vendors.name: Rapid7, Inc.
risk_coverage_vendors.product_name: Rapid7 InsightVM (Active Risk / CISA KEV) + InsightIDR (Attacker Behavior Analytics)
risk_coverage_vendors.what_they_do: Rapid7 InsightVM provides CISA KEV-integrated vulnerability management with Active Risk scoring that automatically incorporates the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog into prioritised remediation, while InsightIDR's Attacker Behavior Analytics (ABA) provides pre-built Exchange Server OWA exploitation detections used by Managed Detection and Response analysts to identify zero-day compromises.
risk_coverage_vendors.fit_rationale: Rapid7 InsightVM's Active Risk scoring automatically incorporates the CISA KEV catalog — directly supporting MC-REG-004's requirement to track FCEB agency remediation obligations for CVE-2026-42897 as a KEV-listed vulnerability. InsightIDR's pre-built ABA detections for Exchange OWA exploitation (documented in a published blog using real customer incident data) support MC-CYB-036 monitoring, though Exchange Health Checker EEMS status verification is a separate Microsoft-provided tool that Rapid7 does not replace.

capability_claims.claim: Rapid7 InsightIDR observed a notable increase in exploitation of Microsoft Exchange through existing detections in InsightIDR's Attacker Behavior Analytics (ABA), with the Managed Detection and Response team identifying multiple related compromises of on-premises Microsoft Exchange servers with OWA accessible to the public internet.
capability_claims.source_span: Rapid7 has observed a notable increase in the exploitation of Microsoft Exchange through existing detections in InsightIDR's Attacker Behavior Analytics (ABA).

capability_claims.claim: Rapid7 InsightIDR provided proactive, actionable steps to customers to thwart Exchange zero-day attacks before Microsoft's public acknowledgement, with the MDR team identifying attacker techniques, source IPs, and tools used, including detection of webshell uploads to OWA paths.
capability_claims.source_span: Within a very short period of time, our analysts were able to identify how the attackers were executing commands, where they were coming from, and what tools they were using.

capability_claims.claim: Rapid7 InsightVM's Active Risk scoring incorporates the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog alongside AttackerKB, Metasploit, and ExploitDB to provide threat-aware vulnerability risk scores, directly surfacing KEV-listed Exchange vulnerabilities for prioritised remediation.
capability_claims.source_span: Active Risk uses CVSS scores along with intelligence from threat feeds like AttackerKB, Metasploit, ExploitDB, Project Heisenberg, CISA KEV list,

mitigation_mechanism.summary: Rapid7 InsightVM identifies Exchange Server 2016, 2019, and SE instances across the on-premises estate and automatically tags CVE-2026-42897 (as a CISA KEV-listed vulnerability) with elevated Active Risk scores — supporting MC-REG-004 by providing FCEB agencies with a prioritised, evidence-based remediation queue. InsightIDR's ABA detections flag the behavioural indicators of Exchange OWA exploitation (webshell uploads to OWA paths, IIS log anomalies, credential-dumping post-exploitation) that correlate with EEMS mitigation verification failures, supporting MC-CYB-036 by alerting administrators to compromises on servers where EEMS M2.1.x has not been verified. The MDR service layer provides 24/7 analyst oversight of these detections.
grounding_spans: Rapid7 has observed a notable increase in the exploitation of Microsoft Exchange through existing detections in InsightIDR's Attacker Behavior Analytics (ABA).

Exposing web services to the public internet is a common practice for customers with on-premise instances of Microsoft Exchange to provide their users with email services over the web through Outlook Web Access (OWA).

enabling_features.what_it_does: Pre-built detection rules in InsightIDR flagging Exchange OWA exploitation patterns including webshell execution and post-exploitation credential dumping, with documented real-world Exchange zero-day detections.
enabling_features.source_span: Attacker Tool - China Chopper Webshell Executing Commands

enabling_features.feature: Managed Detection and Response (MDR) for Exchange
enabling_features.what_it_does: 24/7 MDR SOC analysts monitor InsightIDR alerts for Exchange OWA exploitation, providing proactive customer notification before public vulnerability acknowledgement.
enabling_features.source_span: The Managed Detection and Response (MDR) identified multiple, related compromises in the past 72 hours.

enabling_features.feature: Active Risk Scoring with CISA KEV Integration
enabling_features.what_it_does: Automatically incorporates CISA KEV catalog into vulnerability risk scores, tagging CVE-2026-42897 across all Exchange Server instances in the estate for prioritised remediation tracking.
enabling_features.source_span: Active Risk uses CVSS scores along with intelligence from threat feeds like AttackerKB, Metasploit, ExploitDB, Project Heisenberg, CISA KEV list, and other third-party dark web sources to provide security teams with threat-aware vulnerability risk scores on scale of 0-1000.

workflow_steps.steps:
1. Deploy InsightVM agents on all Exchange servers: Install Rapid7 InsightVM agents on every on-premises Exchange Server 2016, 2019, and SE node to enable continuous vulnerability scanning.
2. Enable Active Risk scoring: Set Active Risk as the risk scoring strategy to automatically surface CVE-2026-42897 (CISA KEV-listed) at the top of the remediation queue across all Exchange nodes.
3. Generate CISA KEV remediation report: Use InsightVM's CISA KEV tag filter to generate a per-server remediation report showing CVE-2026-42897 status — supporting the MC-REG-004 attestation package for each FCEB agency Exchange server.
4. Deploy InsightIDR with Exchange ABA detections: Enable InsightIDR's Attacker Behavior Analytics (ABA) library on Exchange servers, activating pre-built detections for OWA exploitation indicators including webshell execution.
5. Monitor ABA alerts for EEMS verification gaps: Configure InsightIDR alerts for Exchange OWA exploitation patterns to flag servers where exploitation activity is detected but EEMS M2.1.x status has not been confirmed.
6. Engage MDR analysts: For FCEB agencies, engage Rapid7's MDR service to provide 24/7 monitoring of InsightIDR alerts during the CISA KEV remediation window (through 2026-05-29).
grounding_spans: Rapid7 has observed a notable increase in the exploitation of Microsoft Exchange through existing detections in InsightIDR's Attacker Behavior Analytics (ABA).
This information allowed Rapid7 to provide proactive, actionable steps to our customers to thwart the attack.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Exchange Health Checker EEMS M2.1.x status verification (MC-CYB-036 core requirement)
dimension_coverage.note: Rapid7 does not replace the Exchange Health Checker script (aka.ms/ExchangeHealthChecker) required by MC-CYB-036 to verify EEMS M2.1.x applied status; that is a Microsoft-provided tool the Exchange administrator must run directly.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Rapid7 has observed a notable increase in the exploitation of Microsoft Exchange through existing detections in InsightIDR's Attacker Behavior Analytics (ABA).

dimension_coverage.dimension: Detection of exploitation on un-mitigated Exchange nodes
dimension_coverage.note: InsightIDR ABA detections flag active exploitation on Exchange OWA servers, providing an alerting layer for servers where EEMS mitigation has not been verified.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: analysts identified that attackers had successfully compromised several systems and noted that they were all on-premise Microsoft Exchange servers with web services accessible to the public Internet.

mitigation_limits.limit: Rapid7 does not perform GDPR Article 33 breach assessments or mailbox forwarding rule audits; its scope is vulnerability management and threat detection.
grounding_spans: This information allowed Rapid7 to provide proactive, actionable steps to our customers to thwart the attack.

risk_coverage_vendors.score_rationale: Rapid7 scores highly because verified capability claims document pre-patch Exchange OWA zero-day detection via InsightIDR ABA with MDR-identified webshell uploads and attacker techniques, directly matching the incident scenario, while InsightVM's CISA KEV integration addresses MC-CYB-036 prioritisation; minor deductions reflect that CVE-2026-42897 post-dates Rapid7's documented Exchange responses (creating an inference gap), evidence lacks named customer citations, and Rapid7's market prominence introduces modest reputation-halo risk.

risk_coverage_vendors.fit_rationale: OneTrust Incident & Breach Response is purpose-built to manage GDPR Article 33 breach notification obligations, with breach notification laws — including the 72-hour supervisory authority notification requirement — built directly into the platform via Databreachpedia™. This directly addresses MC-REG-016's requirement to assess whether CVE-2026-42897 mailbox session compromise constitutes a personal data breach and to file a supervisory authority notification within 72 hours if it does.

capability_claims.claim: OneTrust Incident & Breach Response enables organisations to centrally manage, analyse, investigate, report and monitor the full incident and breach lifecycle and is powered by Databreachpedia™ Global Law Engine, integrating hundreds of region-specific breach notification laws directly into the platform.

capability_claims.source_span: With breach notification laws ranging from 72 hours to more than 30 days, privacy and security teams need a flexible solution to centrally manage response plans across the globe

capability_claims.claim: OneTrust Incident & Breach Response automatically tailors response workflows based on impacted regions' breach notification laws and can integrate into a company's SIEM and ITSM tools for automated breach intake.

mitigation_mechanism.summary: OneTrust Incident & Breach Response enables the Data Protection Officer or privacy lead to initiate a structured GDPR Article 33 breach-likelihood assessment directly within the platform following the CVE-2026-42897 disclosure. The Databreachpedia™ engine automatically applies GDPR Article 33's 72-hour supervisory authority notification requirement based on the jurisdiction of affected EU-resident mailbox holders, generating a pre-configured assessment workflow. If the DPO determines that a personal data breach cannot be ruled out, the platform generates a supervisory authority notification document and tracks the filing deadline with automated reminders. The full audit trail — breach/no-breach decision, rationale, and notification filing reference — is retained as a GDPR accountability record.

enabling_features.source_span: The solution can integrate into a company's related applications, such as data loss prevention, threat intelligence, GRC, SIEM and ITSM tools.

workflow_steps.steps:
1. Intake the CVE-2026-42897 incident: Receive the incident in OneTrust via SIEM integration (or manual intake) on 2026-05-14 when disclosure and active exploitation were confirmed.
2. Identify EU-resident mailbox holders: Using OneTrust's data mapping integration, identify all EU-resident data subjects whose mailboxes are hosted on the affected Exchange Server 2016, 2019, or SE deployments.
3. Initiate Databreachpedia™ GDPR Article 33 assessment: Select the GDPR assessment template for each EU jurisdiction where EU-resident mailbox holders are served, triggering the automated 72-hour notification workflow.
4. Conduct breach-likelihood assessment: Evaluate whether CVE-2026-42897 exploitation — enabling session-token theft and mailbox impersonation — constitutes a personal data breach for EU-resident mailbox holders, documenting the rationale within the platform.
5. File supervisory authority notification (if applicable): If a breach cannot be ruled out, generate the Article 33 notification document from the OneTrust platform and file it with the competent supervisory authority within 72 hours of the breach-likelihood determination.
6. Retain accountability record: Save the completed assessment outcome, notification filing reference, and remediation actions as an accountability record within OneTrust for regulator review.

risk_coverage_vendors.score_rationale: OneTrust precisely covers the regulatory notification lifecycle (MC-REG-016 / GDPR Art.33 72-hour window) making it a strong fit for the enabler role on that narrow compliance dimension, but the incident is a CYB severity-4 zero-day XSS exploit where the primary gap is technical detection/containment rather than breach-notification workflow, the evidence base is marketing-page product claims without named customer references or third-party validation, and the incident category mismatch (active exploitation response vs. notification management) substantially limits D3 and overall score.

incident_handler_vendors.name: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.
incident_handler_vendors.product_name: CrowdStrike Falcon Complete / CrowdStrike Incident Response Services
incident_handler_vendors.what_they_do: CrowdStrike provides Falcon Complete managed detection and response and on-demand Incident Response Services that have documented real-world experience detecting, containing, and remediating Microsoft Exchange Server OWA zero-day exploitation, including webshell removal, IIS log forensics, and post-exploitation artefact remediation.
incident_handler_vendors.handles_scenario: CrowdStrike Falcon Complete has documented a real-world mass Exchange Server OWA zero-day exploitation response — detecting webshells dropped via IIS, containing compromised servers, removing malicious artefacts, and conducting root cause analysis via IIS and ECP log forensics — exactly matching the CVE-2026-42897 incident scenario.
incident_handler_vendors.fit_rationale: CrowdStrike has published a detailed engagement disclosure (Falcon Complete Stops Microsoft Exchange Server Zero-Day Exploits) documenting real-world IR response to Exchange OWA zero-day exploitation with specific artefact types, containment actions, and forensic techniques that directly match the CVE-2026-42897 scenario, plus a documented 24/7/365 IR retainer service with 100,000+ annual IR hours.

capability_claims.claim: CrowdStrike Falcon Complete detected and disrupted a mass exploitation campaign of Microsoft Exchange Server OWA zero-day vulnerabilities, identifying compromised Exchange application pools via the MSExchangeOWAAppPool process tree, removing webshells, and containing impacted hosts — while the permanent patch was unavailable.
capability_claims.source_span: OverWatch flagged the "W3WP.EXE" process as malicious due to an observed attempt to exploit the Exchange application pool named "MSExchangeOWAAppPool."

capability_claims.claim: CrowdStrike IR conducts IIS log forensics, ECP server log analysis, and endpoint activity monitor (EAM) queries to identify the full scope of Exchange OWA exploitation, including webshell file writes and post-exploitation commands executed under W3WP.exe.
capability_claims.source_span: Falcon Complete immediately began pulling the IIS logs from the impacted hosts to search for artifacts in an attempt to confirm the initial entry vector.

mitigation_mechanism.summary: Following detection of CVE-2026-42897 exploitation, CrowdStrike deploys its Critical Escalation Playbook: Falcon Complete analysts network-contain compromised Exchange servers, use Real-Time Response (RTR) to enumerate and remove webshells from IIS directories, analyse IIS and ECP server logs to reconstruct the exploitation chain, and conduct endpoint memory forensics to identify injected DLLs. The OverWatch proactive threat-hunting team provides early visibility into novel exploitation patterns — as it did for the Exchange zero-days in 2021 — before threat signatures are publicly available. The IR service also monitors for post-exploitation artefacts including email forwarding rules consistent with MC-DAT-013.

enabling_features.feature: Real-Time Response (RTR) for Remote Artefact Removal
enabling_features.what_it_does: Enables Falcon Complete analysts to remotely connect to compromised Exchange servers, enumerate webshell files, remove malicious artefacts, and execute remediation commands without physical access.
enabling_features.source_span: Using the Real Time Response capability of the Falcon agent, Falcon Complete connected to the impacted hosts to begin the collection and remediation of malicious artifacts.

enabling_features.feature: Endpoint Activity Monitor (EAM) for Webshell Detection
enabling_features.what_it_does: EAM queries search for NewScriptWritten events targeting .ASPX file writes in Exchange OWA directories, enabling rapid identification of post-exploitation webshell drops.
enabling_features.source_span: A lucrative initial pivot point for investigating intrusions involving webshells is a search to identify recent files written to disk with the ".ASPX" file extension.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Analysts pull and analyse Exchange IIS HttpProxy logs and ECP server logs to reconstruct the OWA exploitation chain, including attacker POST requests to exploit paths and post-compromise artefact activity.
enabling_features.source_span: Falcon Complete immediately began pulling the IIS logs from the impacted hosts to search for artifacts in an attempt to confirm the initial entry vector.

enabling_features.what_it_does: 24/7 proactive threat hunting team that identified novel Exchange zero-day exploitation patterns within days of active exploitation beginning — before Microsoft public acknowledgement — providing early warning for CVE-2026-42897-type scenarios.
enabling_features.source_span: Starting Sunday, Feb. 28, the Falcon OverWatch team of threat hunters saw the first signs of a novel intrusion. They observed instances of an unknown attacker gaining unauthorized access to on-premises Microsoft Exchange application pools running on several hosts across multiple customer environments, and immediately commenced notifying affected organizations.

workflow_steps.steps:
1. Initial detection and triage (within hours of disclosure): Falcon sensor detects W3WP.exe spawning shells or OWAAppPool anomalies; Falcon Complete follows Critical Escalation Playbook to notify the customer and begin investigation.
2. Network containment: Impacted Exchange servers are network-contained via Falcon to prevent further exploitation and lateral movement while investigation proceeds.
3. Webshell enumeration and removal: RTR analysts execute EAM queries for .ASPX NewScriptWritten events in Exchange OWA directories and use RTR to delete identified webshells and associated DLL artefacts.
4. IIS and ECP log forensics: Analysts pull IIS HttpProxy and ECP server logs to reconstruct the full exploitation chain, identifying all compromised mailboxes and attacker-executed commands.
5. Post-exploitation artefact audit: Analysts search Exchange audit logs for attacker-planted forwarding rules, impersonation rules, and any new accounts created during the compromise window (supporting MC-DAT-013 and MC-CYB-001).
6. Remediation and release from containment: After webshells and persistence mechanisms are removed and EEMS/EOMT mitigations are confirmed, servers are released from containment and customers receive a full incident report.

incident_handler_vendors.score_rationale: Two verified capability claims document CrowdStrike Falcon Complete's direct, operational response to Exchange OWA zero-day mass exploitation — including MSExchangeOWAAppPool process-tree detection, webshell removal, and IIS/ECP log forensics — an exact scenario match that drives near-maximum D1 and D3 scores, with D2 slightly discounted for absence of named customer references and D4 modestly penalised given CrowdStrike's brand weight in MDR/IR markets.

incident_handler_vendors.what_they_do: Mandiant provides incident response and compromise assessment services for breaches and active exploitation scenarios, deploying on-call incident responders globally within hours to conduct in-depth attack analysis, containment, forensic investigation, and full breach remediation.
incident_handler_vendors.handles_scenario: Mandiant Incident Response Services respond to active Exchange Server OWA zero-day exploitation by deploying on-call incident responders to perform in-depth attack analysis, contain the threat, conduct forensic investigation of compromised mailboxes and IIS logs, and recover business operations — with a Retainer option providing 2-hour response time.
incident_handler_vendors.fit_rationale: Mandiant's IR service page documents on-call incident responders who immediately begin triage, minimise business impact through methodical investigation and containment, and deliver rapid recovery with full breach remediation — directly matching the post-exploitation IR requirements for CVE-2026-42897, including IIS log forensics, mailbox session analysis, and GDPR-relevant breach scope determination.

mitigation_mechanism.summary: Following CVE-2026-42897 exploitation, Mandiant deploys on-call incident responders to conduct in-depth forensic analysis of Exchange IIS logs, ECP logs, and mailbox session data to identify the scope of compromise — including which mailboxes were impersonated, which session tokens were stolen, and whether attacker-planted forwarding rules exist (MC-DAT-013, MC-CYB-001). The Compromise Assessment service specifically searches for previously unseen attacker activity and identifies configuration weaknesses, supporting both the forensic investigation of the active exploitation and the GDPR Article 33 breach scope determination (MC-REG-016). workflow_steps.steps:
1. Retainer activation (within 2 hours of breach confirmation): Engage Mandiant Retainer to activate pre-negotiated IR terms and deploy on-call incident responders immediately upon confirming CVE-2026-42897 active exploitation.
2. Triage and scoping: Incident responders review Exchange IIS logs, OWA authentication logs, and Falcon/EDR telemetry to identify compromised servers, affected mailboxes, and attacker-executed commands.
3. Containment: Isolate compromised Exchange servers from network where possible, revoke active OWA sessions, and block attacker source IPs at the perimeter.
4. Forensic investigation: Conduct in-depth IIS log and ECP server log analysis to reconstruct the CVE-2026-42897 exploit chain, enumerate webshell locations, and identify all post-exploitation artefacts including forwarding rules.
5. GDPR breach scope determination: Provide forensic findings to the DPO to support the GDPR Article 33 breach-likelihood assessment (MC-REG-016), documenting which EU-resident mailboxes were accessed and what data may have been exfiltrated.
6. Remediation and recovery: Guide Exchange administrator through EEMS Health Checker verification, EOMT execution for air-gapped nodes, forwarding rule revocation, and MFA enforcement, then provide final incident report with best-practice recommendations.

read_across.if_you_operate_x_then_y: If you operate on-premises Microsoft Exchange Server 2016, 2019, or Subscription Edition with OWA internet-accessible, you should immediately verify EEMS mitigation M2.1.x status via the Exchange Health Checker script, apply EOMT manually on any air-gapped nodes, audit all mailboxes for anomalous forwarding rules created since 2026-05-14, and enrol Exchange 2016/2019 in ESU Period 2 to receive the permanent patch in the next 24-72 hours.

incident.summary: On approximately 20 May 2026 (reported by The Moscow Times within the sweep window), Russian authorities declared cleanup operations complete at the Rosneft-operated Tuapse refinery on the Black Sea coast, following a series of Ukrainian drone strikes beginning 16 April that triggered massive fires, a 7–77 km oil slick, black rain containing benzene and xylene, and contamination of the Tuapse River and Black Sea coastal waters. Environmental experts and volunteers have publicly disputed official claims that the damage is contained, stating authorities are underreporting the ecological harm. The refinery — with annual throughput of approximately 12 million metric tonnes — remains offline, with dead dolphins, birds, and fish reported along the coastline near Sochi and Anapa.
incident.severity_rationale: Catastrophic multi-media contamination (air, sea, river, soil) across a major coastal region; refinery indefinitely offline; wildlife mass deaths; ~60,000 residents exposed to toxic compounds; Russian government information suppression; disputed cleanup claims by independent experts.
incident.threat_actor: Ukrainian Armed Forces (drone strikes attributed to Ukrainian UAVs per multiple sources)

secondary_mappings.why: Mass deaths of dolphins, waterfowl, and marine fauna documented along up to 77 km of Black Sea coastline, with rare bird species affected.
The Tuapse refinery — Russia's only major Black Sea refinery — has suspended operations indefinitely, forcing diversion of fuel exports to Novorossiysk.
Russian authorities have been publicly accused of concealing the scale of damage; a journalist was detained and state media suppressed early reporting.
Residents of Tuapse (pop. ~60,000) were exposed to toxic benzene, xylene, and soot; schools closed and evacuation orders issued.
The environmental disaster is a direct consequence of the Russo-Ukrainian war, with Ukrainian drone strikes deliberately targeting Russian energy export infrastructure.

control_objectives.statement: Ensure Rosneft has verified, independent environmental damage baselines for the Tuapse River and Black Sea coastal zone before any official cleanup certification is accepted as accurate.
Assess and document biodiversity harm to marine and avian species in the Black Sea coastal corridor from Tuapse to Anapa and Sochi following confirmed dead dolphins, birds, and fish.
Maintain business continuity capacity for the 12-million-tonne Tuapse refinery throughput across alternative Rosneft export routes while the facility remains indefinitely offline.
Ensure critical infrastructure resilience for the Tuapse refinery-terminal complex against continued attritional Ukrainian drone strikes documented on at least four occasions since 16 April 2026.
Manage reputational risk arising from the disputed 'cleanup complete' declaration against independent expert and volunteer accounts of ongoing ecological contamination.
Protect the health and safety of Tuapse's population of approximately 60,000 exposed to benzene, xylene, and soot at levels up to three times safe limits during and after the strikes.

Monitor and manage geopolitical conflict escalation risk to Rosneft's Black Sea energy infrastructure as Ukraine's documented attritional drone campaign continues to target refineries and export terminals.

master_controls.statement: Rosneft must commission independent water and soil sampling of the Tuapse River and Black Sea coastal zone up to 77 km from the refinery to establish a verified contamination baseline contradicting or confirming the 20 May 2026 official cleanup declaration.

vendors_covering.name: CEDRE (Centre of Documentation, Research and Experimentation on Accidental Water Pollution)

master_controls.statement: Deploy emergency containment boom and skimmer assets for any residual hydrocarbon release from Tuapse River into the Black Sea, given that rainfall overtopped containment barriers during the April strikes.

Implement a long-term ecological monitoring programme covering benzene and xylene soil accumulation along the 70-km contaminated Krasnodar coastline, with data published independently of Rosprirodnadzor's official reporting.

vendors_covering.name: CEDRE (Centre of Documentation, Research and Experimentation on Accidental Water Pollution)

master_controls.statement: Activate Rosneft's oil-spill notification obligations under the Bucharest Convention on Protection of the Black Sea, given the transboundary slick reaching coastal areas beyond Tuapse municipality as documented by satellite imagery on 26 April 2026.

Initiate an emergency biodiversity impact assessment covering cetacean (dead dolphins reported near Sochi), avian (oiled waterfowl documented at rehabilitation centres in Anapa), and ichthyofauna mortality along the affected Black Sea coastline.
Establish wildlife rehabilitation capacity at Anapa rehabilitation centres and Tuapse coastal staging areas proportionate to confirmed multi-species mortality from the April 2026 spill event.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: Rosneft must commission independent water and soil sampling
adaptive_controls.statement: Rosneft must engage a qualified independent environmental laboratory — accredited outside the Russian federal regulatory system — to collect water and sediment samples at a minimum of 15 georeferenced stations along the Tuapse River from the refinery outfall to its Black Sea mouth and at 10-km intervals along the coastline up to 77 km from the Tuapse refinery, with all sampling completed no later than 30 days from the 20 May 2026 official cleanup declaration. Samples must be analysed for petroleum hydrocarbons, benzene, xylene, and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAH), with chain-of-custody documentation and full analytical results published in an unredacted report accessible to Rosprirodnadzor, Black Sea NGO observers, and the relevant Bucharest Convention secretariat. Rosneft's Chief Environmental Officer bears accountability for commissioning, and completion is evidenced by publication of the laboratory report with GPS coordinates, collection timestamps, and accreditation credentials for the testing facility.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The adaptive control operationalises the disputed 20 May 2026 cleanup declaration as the specific trigger and clock-start, names the exact spatial scope (Tuapse River outfall to 77 km coastal extent) derived from reported slick dimensions, and mandates external accreditation outside Russian federal oversight to address the publicly disputed underreporting by Rosprirodnadzor — none of which is captured in the generic independent-sampling master control.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: Deploy emergency containment boom and skimmer assets for
adaptive_controls.statement: Rosneft must maintain continuous deployment of oil-containment boom across the full width of the Tuapse River mouth and at the two primary storm-water outfall channels from the Tuapse refinery site, supplemented by at least two skimmer units capable of recovering a combined minimum of 50 cubic metres per hour of surface hydrocarbon, until independent water-quality sampling (see AC-ENV-001-ESG-01) confirms total petroleum hydrocarbon concentrations at the river mouth fall below Russian fishery-water standards for three consecutive weekly measurements. The Tuapse Refinery Emergency Response Manager is accountable for daily boom-integrity inspections, with photographic logs timestamped and retained as completion evidence; any gap in boom continuity exceeding two hours must trigger an immediate written escalation to Rosneft's central HSE directorate.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The adaptive control specifies the Tuapse River mouth and refinery storm-water outfalls as the precise deployment points — reflecting confirmed contamination of these specific watercourses — and ties the stand-down criterion to independently verified water-quality thresholds rather than the disputed official declaration, addressing the demonstrated failure of authority-led containment verification following the April 2026 strikes.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: Implement a long-term ecological monitoring programme covering benzene
adaptive_controls.statement: Rosneft must establish a five-year soil and groundwater monitoring programme along the approximately 70-km Krasnodar coastline from Tuapse to the Anapa district, with semi-annual sampling for benzene, xylene, and total petroleum hydrocarbons at a minimum of 20 fixed transects installed no later than 60 days from 20 May 2026; all data must be submitted concurrently to an independent scientific repository — such as the Black Sea Commission's data network — and published on Rosneft's corporate environmental portal within 30 days of each sampling round, in a format that allows direct comparison with the reported air-concentration exceedances of up to three times safe limits recorded during the April 2026 strikes. The programme director must be an external environmental scientist with no contractual dependency on Rosprirodnadzor approvals, and programme completion per cycle is evidenced by timestamped data uploads to the independent repository prior to any Rosneft or Rosprirodnadzor public statement on contamination status.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The adaptive control anchors the monitoring to the specific 70-km affected coastline between Tuapse and Anapa and to the confirmed benzene/xylene air exceedances as the baseline-setting event, and explicitly routes data publication outside Rosprirodnadzor's reporting chain — a safeguard the generic master control does not prescribe — directly responding to the publicly disputed underreporting following the April 2026 incident.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: Activate Rosneft's oil-spill notification obligations under the Bucharest
adaptive_controls.statement: Rosneft, through the Russian Ministry of Natural Resources as the competent national authority under the Bucharest Convention on the Protection of the Black Sea Against Pollution, must submit a formal transboundary pollution notification to the Bucharest Convention Permanent Secretariat — covering the reported 77-km oil slick, benzene and xylene atmospheric deposition, and Tuapse River coastal-water contamination — within 15 calendar days of publication of these adaptive controls, and must provide updated incident reports to the Secretariat at 30-day intervals until independent water-quality data (per AC-ENV-001-ESG-01) confirms contamination within municipal Tuapse boundaries. The notification package must include the satellite-imagery-documented slick extent of 26 April 2026, the reported 7,000–10,000 square-metre offshore slick area, and the species mortality data from the Sochi–Anapa coastline, and Rosneft's Head of Regulatory Affairs is accountable for ensuring submission with delivery confirmation as the evidential record.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The adaptive control names the specific Bucharest Convention notification pathway, keys the trigger to the confirmed transboundary slick extent supported by 26 April satellite imagery, and specifies the exact incident data packages — slick dimensions, atmospheric contamination metrics, and mortality observations — that the generic spill-notification obligation does not enumerate, ensuring the submission reflects the documented scale of the April 2026 multi-strike event rather than a pro forma filing.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: Initiate an emergency biodiversity impact assessment covering cetacean
adaptive_controls.statement: Rosneft must commission an emergency biodiversity impact assessment of the Black Sea coastline from Tuapse to Anapa, conducted by a team that includes a cetacean specialist, a marine ornithologist, and an ichthyologist, with field surveys initiated within 14 days of these adaptive controls and a preliminary report delivered within 45 days; the assessment must quantify cetacean strandings (documented dead dolphins near Sochi), avian oiling (reported at Anapa rehabilitation centres), and fish mortality along the affected coastline as separate, species-group-specific impact registers rather than a single aggregated figure. Methodology must comply with IUCN rapid-assessment protocols, the full data set must be shared with the Black Sea Commission's Advisory Group on the Black Sea Ecosystem, and Rosneft's Environmental Affairs Director is accountable for contracting and delivery, with the signed contract and field-survey commencement logs as initiation evidence.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The adaptive control disaggregates the mortality assessment into the three specific taxonomic groups evidenced in reported facts — cetaceans near Sochi, oiled waterfowl at Anapa, and fish mortality — preventing aggregation that could obscure species-specific harm, and sets the 14-day field-survey initiation window against the post-20 May declaration timeline when carcass evidence may be degrading, neither of which the generic biodiversity-assessment master control specifies.

adaptive_controls.parent_mc_title: Establish wildlife rehabilitation capacity at Anapa rehabilitation centres
adaptive_controls.statement: Rosneft must fund and operationalise wildlife rehabilitation capacity at the Anapa rehabilitation centre and at a forward staging area within 20 km of the Tuapse refinery coastline, scaled to admit and treat a minimum of 200 oiled seabirds and 10 cetaceans concurrently, with both facilities fully staffed and equipped — including bird-washing stations, cetacean triage pools, and veterinary personnel experienced in petroleum toxicology — within 10 calendar days of these adaptive controls given that dolphin, bird, and fish mortality along the Sochi–Anapa coastline is already reported and the 20 May 2026 cleanup declaration remains disputed. Rosneft must provide weekly intake and outcome reports (admissions, treatments, releases, mortalities by species) to the independent biodiversity assessment team (per AC-ENV-019-ESG-01) and to the Black Sea Commission, with the operational readiness certificate of each facility as the evidence of initial compliance.
adaptive_controls.rationale: The adaptive control specifies minimum concurrent treatment capacity calibrated to reported multi-species mortality across the Sochi–Anapa coastline and ties the 10-day stand-up deadline to the already-elapsed post-strike period and disputed cleanup declaration, preventing the generic rehabilitation-capacity obligation from being interpreted as a forward-planning measure rather than an immediate-response requirement given documented ongoing mortality.

risk_coverage_vendors.name: Oil Spill Response Limited (OSRL)
risk_coverage_vendors.product_name: Wildlife Oil Spill Response Services – GOWRS and Sea Alarm
risk_coverage_vendors.what_they_do: OSRL provides wildlife response services covering oiled birds, mammals, and reptiles through partnerships with Sea Alarm (strategic and tactical advice) and the Global Oiled Wildlife Response System (GOWRS) (in-field expert assessment teams), with pre-positioned wildlife response equipment for rapid deployment.
risk_coverage_vendors.fit_rationale: OSRL's documented wildlife response service deploys a four-person expert assessment team via GOWRS within four days and provides Sea Alarm advisors for strategic cetacean, avian, and fish mortality response guidance, directly addressing the MC-ENV-019 requirement for an emergency biodiversity impact assessment covering cetaceans, avian species, and ichthyofauna. Coverage is partial because GOWRS delivers an initial assessment and report rather than the full 45-day IUCN rapid-assessment protocol engagement mandated by AC-ENV-019-ESG-01.

capability_claims.claim: OSRL's partnership with Sea Alarm gives members anytime expert wildlife response support, with one advisor deployable onsite and another providing remote technical guidance.
capability_claims.source_span: OSRL's partnership with Sea Alarm gives members anytime expert wildlife response support, with one advisor deployable onsite and another providing remote technical guidance.

capability_claims.claim: GOWRS delivers an expert-led wildlife assessment in four days, guiding OSRL members on response needs, local capacity, and next steps for full-scale mobilisation.
capability_claims.source_span: GOWRS delivers an expert-led wildlife assessment in four days, guiding OSRL members on response needs, local capacity, and next steps for full-scale mobilisation.

capability_claims.claim: OSRL wildlife services include monitoring wildlife in real-time, prioritising clean-up efforts to prevent oil from reaching sensitive habitats, deterring animals from entering oiled areas, and rehabilitating affected species, with pre-planning and early intervention essential to ensure an effective response.
capability_claims.source_span: This includes monitoring wildlife in real-time, prioritising clean-up efforts to prevent oil from reaching sensitive habitats, deterring animals from entering oiled areas, and rehabilitating affected species.

mitigation_mechanism.summary: Upon activation through the OSRL Duty Manager, Sea Alarm provides immediate strategic and tactical advisory on wildlife response management for the cetacean, avian, and fish mortality documented along the Sochi–Anapa coastline. In parallel, GOWRS deploys a four-person expert team within four days to conduct an in-field oiled wildlife assessment, producing a written report with recommendations for full-scale mobilisation including rehabilitation centre capacity. OSRL's pre-positioned Tier 3 wildlife response equipment — covering field collection, medical, cleaning, and rehabilitation needs — is deployed to support initial response operations at the Anapa and Tuapse staging areas.
grounding_spans: OSRL's partnership with Sea Alarm gives members anytime expert wildlife response support, with one advisor deployable onsite and another providing remote technical guidance.
GOWRS delivers an expert-led wildlife assessment in four days, guiding OSRL members on response needs, local capacity, and next steps for full-scale mobilisation.
OSRL's wildlife kits support the first 48 hours of response, with field and rehab equipment pre-positioned globally for rapid deployment when local access is limited.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Provides anytime strategic and tactical wildlife response advice from Sea Alarm, with one advisor deployable onsite and another providing remote guidance to incident management teams.
enabling_features.source_span: OSRL's partnership with Sea Alarm gives members anytime expert wildlife response support, with one advisor deployable onsite and another providing remote technical guidance.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Deploys a four-person expert team within four days to conduct an in-field oiled wildlife assessment covering response needs, local capacity, and next steps.
enabling_features.source_span: GOWRS delivers an expert-led wildlife assessment in four days, guiding OSRL members on response needs, local capacity, and next steps for full-scale mobilisation.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Pre-positioned globally, these wildlife kits support the first 48 hours of response with field and rehabilitation equipment for search, rescue, medical, cleaning, and rehabilitation of oiled wildlife.
enabling_features.source_span: OSRL's wildlife kits support the first 48 hours of response, with field and rehab equipment pre-positioned globally for rapid deployment when local access is limited.

workflow_steps.steps:
1. Activate wildlife response: Contact OSRL Duty Manager to activate wildlife services under core membership coverage for the Black Sea coastal multi-species mortality event.
2. Sea Alarm remote advisory: Sea Alarm immediately provides remote strategic and tactical advice on cetacean, avian, and fish mortality response, advising on priorities and sequencing along the Tuapse–Anapa coastal corridor.
3. Deploy Sea Alarm advisor on-site: Sea Alarm deploys one advisor on-site to integrate with incident management and coordinate multi-stakeholder wildlife response.
4. GOWRS field assessment: GOWRS deploys a four-person expert team within four days to conduct in-field species-group-specific impact assessment covering dead dolphins near Sochi, oiled birds at Anapa, and fish mortality.
5. Tier 3 equipment deployment: OSRL's pre-positioned wildlife response kits are deployed to Anapa rehabilitation centres and Tuapse coastal staging areas for search, rescue, medical, and rehabilitation operations.
6. Full-scale mobilisation: GOWRS written report guides escalation to full-scale international wildlife response resources via the EUROWA European network and GOWRS global network.
grounding_spans: GOWRS delivers an expert-led wildlife assessment in four days, guiding OSRL members on response needs, local capacity, and next steps for full-scale mobilisation.
OSRL's wildlife kits support the first 48 hours of response, with field and rehab equipment pre-positioned globally for rapid deployment when local access is limited.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Cetacean (dead dolphins) assessment and management
dimension_coverage.note: Sea Alarm and GOWRS cover marine mammals as part of wildlife response but OSRL's own pages do not specifically document cetacean stranding response protocols; marine mammal rehabilitation is noted as handled by specialist organisations.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Oil spills can pose a threat to wildlife, with birds, mammals, and reptiles at risk of contamination.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Avian oiling response and rehabilitation at Anapa
dimension_coverage.note: OSRL's wildlife services explicitly cover oiled bird monitoring, deterrence, and rehabilitation through Sea Alarm advisory and GOWRS assessment teams.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: This includes monitoring wildlife in real-time, prioritising clean-up efforts to prevent oil from reaching sensitive habitats, deterring animals from entering oiled areas, and rehabilitating affected species.

dimension_coverage.dimension: Species-group-specific impact registers per IUCN rapid-assessment protocol
dimension_coverage.note: OSRL's GOWRS assessment delivers a written report with recommendations but the pages do not document compliance with IUCN rapid-assessment protocols or production of separate species-group-specific impact registers.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: GOWRS delivers an expert-led wildlife assessment in four days, guiding OSRL members on response needs, local capacity, and next steps for full-scale mobilisation.

mitigation_limits.limit: OSRL's wildlife assessment via GOWRS produces a written report with recommendations rather than a full IUCN rapid-assessment protocol compliant biodiversity impact assessment; the AC-ENV-019-ESG-01 requirement for a 45-day full assessment with cetacean specialist, marine ornithologist, and ichthyologist would require additional specialist contracting.
grounding_spans: GOWRS delivers an expert-led wildlife assessment in four days, guiding OSRL members on response needs, local capacity, and next steps for full-scale mobilisation.

risk_coverage_vendors.score_rationale: OSRL's verified wildlife response capabilities (Sea Alarm, GOWRS, real-time monitoring, habitat protection) map well to ENV controls and a coastal oil spill scenario, but evidence rests on product-page-level claims with no named case studies, the Tuapse incident's geopolitical/access complexity limits practical deployability, and as a well-known industry cooperative its selection carries moderate reputation-halo risk.

risk_coverage_vendors.name: Sea Alarm Foundation
risk_coverage_vendors.product_name: Wildlife Response Services
risk_coverage_vendors.what_they_do: Sea Alarm is an NGO that provides strategic and tactical advisory, on-site management support, and international resource mobilisation for oiled wildlife emergencies globally, with documented response to a large number of marine oil spill incidents involving birds and marine mammals.
risk_coverage_vendors.fit_rationale: Sea Alarm's documented response services directly address the MC-ENV-019 requirement for an emergency biodiversity impact assessment: Sea Alarm provides on-site and remote strategic advisory, identifies and mobilises international experts including cetacean and avian specialists via EUROWA and GOWRS networks, and assists in setting up wildlife response management systems for multi-species mortality events. Coverage is partial because Sea Alarm is an advisory and coordination body rather than the hands-on field assessment team itself.

capability_claims.claim: Sea Alarm has extensive experience in dealing with oiled wildlife emergencies, providing strategic and tactical advice, coaching key individuals, and helping to set up management and information systems when mobilised to a response.
capability_claims.source_span: Having been involved in the response to a large number of oil spill incidents, Sea Alarm has extensive experience in dealing with oiled wildlife emergencies.

capability_claims.claim: Sea Alarm can assist with the identification, mobilisation and integration of international resources such as experts and equipment, with procedures in place to mobilise the world's most experienced resources to help professionalise a local response effort.
capability_claims.source_span: Sea Alarm can assist with the identification, mobilisation and integration of international resources, such as experts and equipment.

capability_claims.claim: Sea Alarm can optimise a response by analysing emerging needs, filling crucial gaps before they become a problem, and making inexperienced personnel feel comfortable and supported, using international networks and experience to make things work faster and more cost-efficiently.
capability_claims.source_span: Sea Alarm can optimise a response by analysing emerging needs, by filling crucial gaps before they become a problem, and by making inexperienced personnel feel comfortable and supported.

mitigation_mechanism.summary: Sea Alarm operates across three escalating mobilisation levels: Level 1 provides remote advisory connecting the client with specialist experts for initial cetacean, avian, and ichthyofauna mortality guidance; Level 2 deploys Sea Alarm staff on-site with one or two experts to conduct an in-field assessment; Level 3 identifies and mobilises full international expert teams via EUROWA (European network with Belgium-based equipment stockpile) and GOWRS (global network). For the Sochi–Anapa cetacean and avian mortality event, Sea Alarm would progress through levels 1 to 3, identifying and integrating cetacean specialists and marine ornithologists into the response team and ensuring wildlife rehabilitation capacity at Anapa centres is adequate.
grounding_spans: Sea Alarm can provide the following three levels of assistance:
Sea Alarm can assist with the identification, mobilisation and integration of international resources, such as experts and equipment.

EUROWA is a European network of European expert organisations with Sea Alarm as its secretariat. The network includes trained experts that can form a response team that can be mobilised to assist a local response.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Escalates wildlife response from remote advice (Level 1) through on-site assessment (Level 2) to full international expert mobilisation (Level 3), based on the scale of the incident.
enabling_features.source_span: Level 1 Response: Sea Alarm provides distant advice and coaching by, for example, bringing parties into contact with experts who can provide further advice.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Activates a trained European network of wildlife rehabilitation organisations with a shared equipment stockpile in Belgium for rapid deployment to Black Sea coastal incidents.
enabling_features.source_span: EUROWA is a European network of European expert organisations with Sea Alarm as its secretariat. The network includes trained experts that can form a response team that can be mobilised to assist a local response.

enabling_features.what_it_does: Connects wildlife response costs to P&I Club and IOPC Fund compensation mechanisms, ensuring that response expenditure can be documented and claimed against Rosneft's insurer or IOPC.
enabling_features.source_span: Sea Alarm has close contacts with key organisations that are part of the international compensation mechanisms, such as P&I Clubs and the International Oil Pollution Compensation Fund.

workflow_steps.steps:
1. Level 1 activation: Contact Sea Alarm via its 24/7 advisory line; Sea Alarm provides immediate remote guidance on cetacean stranding response near Sochi and oiled avian species at Anapa.
2. Expert identification: Sea Alarm identifies cetacean specialists, marine ornithologists, and ichthyologists from its EUROWA and GOWRS networks available for the Black Sea incident.
3. Level 2 on-site deployment: Sea Alarm deploys a small team on-site at the Anapa rehabilitation centre and Tuapse coastal staging area to assess wildlife impact and identify gaps in local capacity.
4. Level 3 mobilisation: Based on the Level 2 assessment, Sea Alarm mobilises EUROWA expert teams and Belgium-stockpiled equipment to supplement Anapa rehabilitation centre capacity.
5. Management system setup: Sea Alarm establishes species-specific information and documentation systems tracking cetacean strandings, avian admissions, and fish mortality as separate registers.
6. Compensation documentation: Sea Alarm coordinates with P&I Clubs and IOPC Fund contacts to ensure wildlife response costs are documented for compensation recovery.
grounding_spans: Sea Alarm can assist with the identification, mobilisation and integration of international resources, such as experts and equipment.

EUROWA is a European network of European expert organisations with Sea Alarm as its secretariat. The network includes trained experts that can form a response team that can be mobilised to assist a local response.

dimension_coverage.note: Sea Alarm's network-based approach can identify and mobilise cetacean specialists, but Sea Alarm itself is a coordination body rather than a cetacean rehabilitation facility.
dimension_coverage.grounding_span: Sea Alarm can assist with the identification, mobilisation and integration of international resources, such as experts and equipment.
